fields.summary: Need privilege change on SF so I can update lab vendor on lab draw opps after approved

content.text: One of the steps in our lab draw process is to go into the patients SF HRT opportunity and change the lab vendor to the appropriate vendor used. I am unable to do this when the opp is approved. Can I request ability to do this? I believe all nurses should be able to do this.  Anita and Sarah P as stake holders. Thank you

requestType.name: General Help Desk Request
requestType.description: Submit a Request or report and issue; fields below are based upon your selection.

content.text: to an appropriate Help Desk team member. They will reach out to you shortly with an update.
Which error message are you getting when you try to edit the closed won opportunity? Can you share a screenshot?

This email, including downloadable files, may contain confidential and/or sensitive personal information and is intended only for the use of the individual and/or the entity for which it is intended. If you are not the intended user, disclosure, copying, use, or distribution of the information included in, and accessed through, is prohibited by federal and state law.
, this issue occurred because the opportunity record was submitted for approval, but I can see it was already approved. Can you check once again if this message persists?
can you check to see if you can do it now
Pedro was working on this. Just wondering if it’s been properly reassigned? We need all the nurses to be able to change the lab vendor on an opportunity after it’s been processed.
I am reassigning this to our new SF Admin and will review it with him tomorrow. I’ll also check in with Idman about this, as I know he is working on permissions issues for all nurses with you.

Sean, can you check if there is a validation rule on Initial HRT or Labs Opp record types that could be limiting the ability to change lab vendor after the Opp is Approved?  Pedro told the clinical team this was fixed but it hasn’t been, they continue to have the same issue.
The lock is coming from the active Opportunity approval process, not from a flow or validation rule.
Record Editability: recordEditability = Admin Or Current Approver
Final Approval Actions >  Record Lock = true

Once a Labs opportunity is approved, Salesforce keeps it locked. Since Jennifer is in Clinical and not an admin or approver, it is expected that she receives the generic record locked error if she tries to edit it.
For Jennifer to update lab vendor on lab draw opps after approved, we will need to either:
Change the approval process so approved Labs opps are not left locked
Add a narrow post-approval edit mechanism for lab vendor

I have investigated this and my findings show this is the expected behavior based on the current Approval Process configuration. The opportunity from your screenshot is in Approved status, and once a Labs opportunity reaches final approval Salesforce keeps the record locked. Because of that, users who are not admins or approvers will see the standard record locked message if they try to make edits after approval.
I forgot to include this part:
I’ll need to determine the best way to grant you approval so you can update lab vendor on lab draw opportunities after it has been approved.

I have crafted a solution where Labs now runs on its own approval process so approved Labs Opportunities are not stuck in that locked state. Once it’s live you should be able to change lab vendor and Lab Name after the Opportunities are approved. Other opportunity types are unchanged.

Added Opportunity_Approval_Process_Labs (Opportunity, Labs record type only, processOrder 1) with finalApprovalRecordLock false. Approvers, emails, and field updates match the old single-process behavior; the step is Labs_Approval_Process_Steps in metadata.
Updated Opportunity_Approval_Process: Labs removed from entry criteria, processOrder 2, finalApprovalRecordLock still true for all opps that still enter this process.
Updated Opportunity_Approval_Process: Genetic Approval Process, processOrder 3

content.text: Hey, do you have a status update on this so I can update the customer?

items.fromString: Parent values: Salesforce(10255)Level 1 values: Other Issues(10303)
items.toString: Parent values: Salesforce(10255)Level 1 values: Permissions/ Access Issues(10295)
Carlin French, Sarah Phonthaphanh, Anita Farrelly

items.fromString: Anita Farrelly, Sarah Phonthaphanh, Carlin French
items.toString: Danielle Cress, Carlin French, Sarah Phonthaphanh, Anita Farrelly

items.fromString:
*Resolution (repo + sandbox* {{10xhealth-sean}})

*Root cause (unchanged):* Approved *Labs* opps hit {{Opportunity_Approval_Process}} with {{finalApprovalRecordLock = true}}, so Clinical users saw standard record lock when editing (e.g. *Lab Name / lab vendor*).

*Fix implemented:* Split approval processes instead of turning off post-approval lock for _all_ record types on one process.

# {{Opportunity_Approval_Process_Labs}} (new, {{processOrder}} 1) — entry *Labs* only, {{finalApprovalRecordLock = false}}, same approvers/steps/actions as the main process (metadata: {{Opportunity.Opportunity_Approval_Process_Labs}}).
# {{Opportunity_Approval_Process}} — *Labs removed* from entry criteria (boolean filter renumbered); {{finalApprovalRecordLock}} restored to {{true}}; {{processOrder}} 2.

Deployed both to *Sean sandbox* (deploy succeeded). *Production not deployed* from this change set.

*Follow-up:* UAT on new Labs submissions / post-approval edit; confirm historical approved opps if any still behave oddly. {{Lab_Ordered_NO_EDIT}} can still block unrelated saves.

Details: {{docs/help-desk-triage/HEAL-32413-triage.md}}

answers.answer:
Hello, 
One of the steps in our lab draw process is to go into the patients SF HRT opportunity and change the lab vendor to the appropriate vendor used. I am unable to do this when the opp is approved. Can I request ability to do this? I believe all nurses should be able to do this.  Anita and Sarah P as stake holders. Thank you
